spec_match.rationale: The IDL API check accurately describes the DOM interface, showing the mapping between the DOM property and HTML attribute.

interactive.rationale: Superb interactive panel with an element-tag picker, reactive getter/setter controllers, a dynamic code generator, and an index reference database table.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Perfectly represents the DOM interface exposure introduced in Chrome 150.

edge_cases.rationale: Clean capability detection with warning alerts if the client browser does not support HTMLElement.prototype.autocorrect, falling back to getAttribute gracefully.

references.rationale: Missing the mandatory reference link to the chromestatus.com/feature/6264645053710336 entry.

design_system.rationale: Clean layout, using consistent flat panel containers and styled monospace blocks.

openQuestions.title: Add missing chromestatus.com reference link
openQuestions.detail: Every concept page must include a link to the associated ChromeStatus entry. Add a link to https://chromestatus.com/feature/6264645053710336 in the references list or crumbs block.

summary: The js-api-inspector is a top-tier, fully interactive DOM debugger for the autocorrect IDL property. However, it fails compliance due to a missing ChromeStatus link.
